sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution urging the Police Accountability Board (PAB), Berkeley Police
Association (BPA), the Berkeley Police Department (BPD) and the City of Berkeley to
complete negotiations on final PAB Regulations.
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
None
sections.current_situation:
In November 2020, Berkeley residents voted to create a Police Accountability Board
(PAB). The PAB would increase the oversight of the Berkeley Police Department and
expand the authority and autonomy of the former Police Review Commission. The PAB
approved interim regulations, to enable the board to begin fulfilling its duties, and
Council approved them in October 2021.
Since that time, final regulations have not come to City Council for approval as
protracted negotiations have continued without stakeholders coming to agreement.
We trust that this negotiation process has been conducted in good faith. However, we
also know that the PAB’s work to create robust oversight of the Berkeley Police
Department will be improved by approving final regulations. In addition, Berkeley’s
Police Officers should have finality and certainty about how personnel complaints will be
handled, and Berkeley residents deserve to know that public safety is being maximized
by a well-functioning relationship between PAB, BPD, and the City in handling
complaints.
This item calls for a resolution to urge completion of the Police Accountability Board
(PAB) Regulations.
sections.background:
In November 2020, the voters of Berkeley overwhelmingly passed Measure II,
amending the City Charter to create the Police Accountability Board (PAB). The
Charter was intended to create robust oversight of the Berkeley Police Department
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7160 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7166
E-Mail: Bblackaby@berkeleyca.gov]
Page 2 of 3
Urging Police Accountability Board Regulations Completion
CONSENT CALENDAR
October 14, 2025
(BPD) by expanding the authority and autonomy of the PAB’s predecessor, the Police
Review Commission (PRC).
The stated purpose of the PAB is “to promote public trust through independent,
objective, civilian oversight of the Berkeley Police Department, provide community
participation in setting and reviewing Police Department policies, practices, and
procedures, and to provide a means for prompt, impartial and fair investigation of
complaints brought by members of the public against sworn employees of the Berkeley
Police Department.” (Charter Article XVIII, Section 125(1).)
The Charter enumerates several powers and duties of the PAB, including the power and
duty “to adopt rules and regulations necessary for the conduct of its business.” (Charter
Article XVIII, Section 125(3)(a)(6).)
The PAB approved the interim regulations, with a few changes, and Council approved
the regulations in October 2021. Council deferred a decision on certain provisions,
however, on the grounds that they would trigger meet and confer negotiations with the
Berkeley Police Association (BPA).
After four years of negotiations, it is necessary to bring final regulations to the Council
for adoption.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
No direct impact
CONTACT PERSON
Councilmember Brent Blackaby Council District 6 510-981-7160

1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
DEADLINE FOR POLICE ACCOUNTABILITY BOARD REGULATIONS
WHEREAS, Berkeley voters overwhelmingly passed Measure II, amending the City
Charter to create the Police Accountability Board (PAB) in November 2020; and
WHEREAS, the PAB would amplify the citizen oversight of the Berkeley Police
Department and expand the authority and autonomy of the former Police Review
Commission (PRC); and
WHEREAS, in October 2021, City Council approved the PAB’s interim regulations,
enabling the Police Accountability Board to begin fulfilling its duties; and
WHEREAS, since that time, 4 years later, final regulations have not come to City
Council for approval as protracted negotiations have continued without stakeholders
coming to agreement; and
WHEREAS, the PAB’s work to create robust civilian oversight of the Berkeley Police
Department would be improved by approving final regulations; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley Police Officers should have finality and certainty about how
personnel complaints will be handled; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley residents deserve to know that public safety is being maximized
by a well-functioning relationship between PAB, BPD and the City in handling
complaints.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
PAB regulations process has been conducted in good faith.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Council urges the Police Accountability Board (PAB),
Berkeley Police Association (BPA), the Berkeley Police Department (BPD) and the City
of Berkeley to complete negotiations on final PAB Regulations.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Council requests a closed session meeting in 2025
to receive updates from the stakeholders on the state of negotiations and any
outstanding issues.
